texto: He likes to know things. He checks out book and record collections when he visits people, looks in medicine cabinets, takes inventory in refrigerators. He eaves drops on conversations at public phone booths. He reads murder victims' mail.
explicacao: Citação de John Sayles sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “He likes to know things. He checks out book and record collections when he visits people, looks i…” articula reflexao, curiosity, likes em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: There was a widespread indignation in the American media. They were saying, 'How can you make a movie during an election that's about politics? What are you doing? Are you trying to influence people's lives?' To which my response was, 'Well, I hope so.'
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de John Sayles que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “There was a widespread indignation in the American media. They were saying, 'How can you make a m…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre vida, esperanca, american, com foco em american e influence.

frase_original: There was a widespread indignation in the American media. They were saying, 'How can you make a movie during an election that's about politics? What are you doing? Are you trying to influence people's lives?' To which my response was, 'Well, I hope so.'
